rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. VS. MICHAEL LIPSON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, July 15, 2019, Line 1 DEFENDANT MICHAEL LIPSON DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT CCP §430.41 requires a declaration reporting that the parties have met and conferred before a demurrer may be filed. Such a declaration was not filed in this instance, so the demurrer is ordered OFF CALENDAR. The parties are ordered to meet and confer immediately and defendant has until September 9, 2019 to respond to the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: STANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC ET AL VS. MEDWHAT.COM INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Complainant Arturo Devesa'S Motion To Compel Cross-Defendants Stanford University, Wallace, Fletcher, Zumwalt Responses For First Set Of Discovery Requests, And For Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 9, 2019, Line 1, Cross Complainant Arturo Devesa'S Motion To Compel Cross-Defendants Stanford University, Wallace, Fletcher, Zumwalt Responses For First Set Of Discovery Requests, And For Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 Of Tentative Ruling) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sborrowman@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (end of part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STANFORD-STARTX FUND, LLC ET AL VS. MEDWHAT.COM INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Cross-Defendants Stanford University, Wallace, Fletcher, Zumwalt Responses For First Set Of Discovery Requests, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 9, 2019, Line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANT ARTURO DEVESA's Motion To Compel Cross-Defendants Stanford University, Wallace, Fletcher, Zumwalt Responses For First Set Of Discovery Requests, And For Sanctions (part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge Scott Borrowman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion DENIED. 1st. Improper service. CCP 1005(b) requires notice 16 court (not calendar) days before the hearing (extended based on manner of service). This motion was served 7/23/2019, making 8/14/2019 the earliest the motion could be heard (assuming personal service). Also, it is unclear there is an agreement for electronic service. CRC 2.251, L.R. 2.11. 2nd. No points and authorities. CRC 3.1113(a). In Reply, Devesa states one "has been submitted in filings with the court" but none is in the online docket. 3rd. No separate statement. CRC 3.1345. In Reply, Devesa states one was filed but none is in the online docket. 4th. The motion is factually unsupported and does not include the discovery requests/responses. 5th. The Notice states Stanford and Zumwalt "have not provided any single written responses or documents in response to any of the Discovery Requests." This is incorrect. As attached to the Ketroser Declaration, Stanford and Wallace provided verified responses. As to Zumwalt, there is no response to the motion but the notice suggests she served objections. The Fayad declaration indicates Fletcher served objections but does not attach them. Objections are responses under the Civil Discovery Act. The Civil Discovery Act "requires that there be a serious effort at negotiation and informal resolution." Townsend v. Sup. Ct. (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 1431, 1438; CCP 2023.010. The responding parties do not have a duty to "invite Mr. Devesa to meet and confer to clarify those requests." (Reply at 4). If Devesa believes the objections and/or responses are insufficient he must meet and confer about them. CCP 2023.020 provides the court "shall impose a monetary sanction" on a party who "fails to confer as required." I find that Devesa failed to confer as required before bringing this motion and misrepresented the status of responses by Stanford and Wallace. Sanctions awarded to Stanford and Wallace for $6,000 ($600/hour as a blended rate for 10 hours). Sanctions awarded to Fletcher for $2,550 ($425/hour for 6 hours). Payable by 8/23/2019. Fletcher's request to stay discovery pending the demurrers is denied. This should be a separate motion.(end of part 1 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT LUJAN VS. DAVID SMALL ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 9, 2019, Line 5 DEFENDANT EDUARDO RALLO DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Eduardo Rallo's demurrer to plaintiff Albert Lujan's second amended complaint (SAC) is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as to all causes of action. A corporate director owes fiduciary duties to the corporation and its stockholders (Corp. Code §309(a)), but Lujan cites no authority holding that such a duty is owed to a co-director. Lujan's reliance on PMC, Inc. v. Kadisha (2000) 78 Cal.App.4th 1368 is misplaced; a director's liability for his tortious acts to third parties does not support the existence of a fiduciary duty to a co-director. Lujan's claims for indemnity and contribution are time-barred by the two-year statute of limitations. (American States Ins. Co. v. National Fire Ins. Co. of Hartford (2011) 202 Cal.App.4th 692, 699.) The statute began to run in 2014, when Lujan started making payments to the tax authorities, and Lujan did not sue until 2018. (SAC 32, 55; People ex rel. Dept. of Transportation v. Sup. Ct. (1980) 26 Cal.3d 744, 755.) Lujan's cause of action for promissory estoppel is likewise time-barred by a two-year statute of limitations. (Newport Harbor Ventures, LLC v. Morris Cerullo World Evangelism (2016) 6 Cal.App.5th 1207, 1224.) Lujan alleges he suffered injury from Rallo's breach of oral promises in 2014, when Lujan started paying tax liabilities, and Lujan was aware Rallo did not intend to honor his promise. (SAC 32, 55.) But Lujan did not sue Rallo until 2018. (Davies v. Krasna (1975) 14 Cal.3d 502, 514.) Because Lujan fails to show he could plead other facts to cure his SAC's fatal defects, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was unopposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
